_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**Q1: Does the workspace have any direct compliance obligation under Regulation (EU) 2023/2405?**
Decided: No. The regulation imposes obligations on aviation fuel suppliers and aircraft operators. Freight forwarders are not named compliance entities. The workspace's obligations are contractual (how it handles SAF surcharge pass-through with airlines and with clients), not regulatory.

*Source: Regulation (EU) 2023/2405, Article 3 (definitions) and Articles 4–5, Official Journal of the European Union, 31 October 2023. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2023/2405/oj/eng*

---

**Q2: Are cargo-only operations (freighter aircraft) subject to the same mandate as passenger operations?**
Decided: Yes. The regulation applies to aircraft operators, defined broadly by reference to departures from EU airports. It is not limited to passenger operations. Airlines operating all-cargo freighter services departing EU airports are within scope.

*Source: Regulation (EU) 2023/2405, Article 3(1) definition of "aircraft operator" and Article 2 scope, Official Journal of the European Union, 31 October 2023. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2023/2405/oj/eng*

---

**Q3: Does the regulation apply to flights departing non-EU airports even when operated by EU-based airlines?**
Decided: The mandate applies at EU airports. Flights departing non-EU airports (e.g., departures from US, UK, or Asian hubs) are not subject to the EU SAF blending mandate, even if the operator is EU-based. However, non-EU jurisdictions may enact their own SAF requirements; these are outside the scope of this document.

*Source: Regulation (EU) 2023/2405, Article 2 (geographical scope) and Article 4, Official Journal of the European Union, 31 October 2023. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2023/2405/oj/eng*

---

**Q4: Are there exemptions for specific cargo types (e.g., humanitarian freight)?**
Research gap: The regulation does not contain cargo-type exemptions from the SAF blending mandate. It does not distinguish between types of freight. Whether humanitarian cargo operators or humanitarian charter operators may access any exemption mechanisms under national competent authority discretion has not been addressed in published authoritative guidance as of the date of this brief. Legal Confirmation Required if the workspace operates humanitarian air freight on EU-origin routes under specific charter or aid organisation arrangements.

*Source: Regulation (EU) 2023/2405, Article 4 (no cargo-type carve-out identified), Official Journal of the European Union, 31 October 2023. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2023/2405/oj/eng*

---
